item.title: Social awareness vital in music education in schools: discussion
item.description: Writers, academics, and musicians said on Saturday that while music is a key cultural element, social awareness is essential in school music education programs......

item.title: Substandard materials alleged in Chuadanga road project
item.description: Allegations of irregularities have intensified regarding the ongoing widening work of the 16-kilometer Jibannagar–Chuadanga road, a project valued at approximately Tk 25 crore, sparking concern and frustration among local residents......

item.title: Students break hunger strike after 72 hrs
item.description: Three students of Dhaka University and North South University who were staging a fast unto death demanding the protection of referendum verdict broke their fast after nearly 72 hours following a request from the families of July martyrs......

item.title: FuelPass portal down
item.description: Motorcyclists trying to collect online cards for purchasing fuel oils on Saturday found the FuelPass portal down for scheduled maintenance, with the mismanagement in distribution of fuel oils and the suffering of consumers to obtain the essential items persisting......

item.title: Hezbollah slams planned Lebanese talks with Israel as strikes kill 10
item.description: Hezbollah on Saturday renewed its rejection of direct negotiations between Israel and Lebanon, where authorities reported 10 people killed in the latest Israeli attacks in the country’s south......

item.title: 3 killed under trains in Jashore, Jamalpur
item.description: At least three people were killed after being hit by trains in Jashore and Jamalpur districts on Saturday......

item.title: One more dies, 888 hospitalised with measles symptoms
item.description: At least 888 new suspected measles cases and one more suspected measles death were reported in the 24 hours until 8:00am Saturday, according to the Directorate General of Health Services......

item.title: DMCH intern doctors give 48-hour to meet 4-point demands
item.description: Intern doctors at Dhaka Medical College Hospital on Saturday issued a 48-hour ultimatum to the authorities to fulfil their four-point demand, including punishment of those involved in the recent assault on physicians......Geographic Reference

item.title: Pir killed, den attacked, set on fire in Kushtia
item.description: An angry mob attacked, vandalised and set fire to the den of a pir in Kushtia’s Daulatpur upazila on Saturday, leaving the pir dead and at least four others injured......

item.title: Govt plans more loans for higher expenditure
item.description: The newly elected Bangladesh Nationalist Party-led government is planning to expand public expenditure in the next financial year by taking more loans from the local and external sources against the backdrop of the revenue collection shortfall......

item.title: US, Iran officials hold direct peace talks in Pakistan
item.description: American and Iranian officials were holding face-to-face talks in Pakistan on Saturday, the most significant since the 1979 Islamic revolution as the foes try to end a war that plunged the Middle East into violence and rocked the world economy......

item.title: New paradigm in engineering education
item.description: HIGHER education across the world is undergoing a gradual yet profound transformation. Advances in digital technology, increased global connectivity and the evolving demands of modern industries are reshaping how universities deliver knowledge. Traditional classroom teaching, once the dominant model of instruction, is increasingly being complemented by......

item.title: CRINK and fracturing global order
item.description: THE war in the Middle East appears to have accelerated the emergence of a new world order, one that is reorganising itself around new poles of power and balance. What once appeared as a loose alignment of convenience is now crystallising into something far more consequential — an emerging formation increasingly described as CRINK: China, Russia, Iran and North Korea......

item.title: Organic: little with no enforcement
item.description: THE label ‘orgaic’ sits on chicken, eggs, rice, flour, mustard oil, spices, vegetables and even everyday staples such as puffed rice, flattened rice and semolina in any upscale supermarket or fashionable neighbourhood shop. Online retailers now market themselves as organic specialists, offering curated baskets of supposedly chemical-free food delivered to the doorstep. For......

item.title: Future of biosecurity
item.description: THE Covid outbreak delivered a painful but invaluable lesson. In an interconnected world, relying almost entirely on external sources for vaccines, therapeutics and diagnostics is incompatible with true health security. When borders closed and rich countries locked up supplies, Bangladesh, like many others, experienced first-hand how fragile global supply chains......

item.title: Need for end to sexual violence in madrassahs
item.description: REPORTS of rape in madrassahs are a long-standing and largely unaddressed concern. A teacher of Mujibnagar Ideal Madrassah and Darul Hifzkhana in Meherpur was arrested on April 10 for allegedly raping a male student. The matter came to light when other students also levelled allegations of similar abuse against the teacher. The arrest took place amid a siege of the......

item.title: Lapsed ordinances, lost opportunities
item.description: THE parliament has repealed seven of the 133 ordinances that the interim administration promulgated during its tenure from August 8, 2024 to February 12, 2026, by enacting four laws and allowed 13 ordinances to lapse as they were not placed as bills within 30 days of the first sitting of the parliament, which went into session on March 12. The parliament has enacted 87 laws......

item.title: Jammat chief for system in which worker’s son may become head of state
item.description: Amir of Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami and leader of opposition in the parliament Shafiqur Rahman on Saturday said that they wanted a system in which the son of a worker could head the government or the state......Bangladesh travel

item.title: Bangladesh, Mauritius discuss boosting bilateral ties
item.description: Foreign minister Khalilur Rahman held talks with his Mauritian counterpart Dhananjay Ramful on strengthening bilateral cooperation on the sidelines of the Ninth Indian Ocean Conference in Mauritius......

item.title: Info minister urges CAs to be prepared for AI challenges
item.description: Information and broadcasting minister Zahir Uddin Swapon on Saturday urged newly qualified chartered accountants to prepare themselves to face the challenges posed by artificial intelligence......

item.title: Book on urban development launched at BUET
item.description: A research book titled ‘Commoning beyond Enclosures: Inhabitations with Rivers and Canals in Basila, Dhaka Western Fringe’ was launched at a programme at the Bangladesh University of Engineering and Technology on Saturday......

item.title: Left leaders call for movement to scrap trade deal with US
item.description: Leaders of the Anti-imperialist and Anti-war Alliance, a newly formed combine, at a protest rally in Dhaka on Saturday called on the government to scrap the ‘unequal’ trade deal signed with the United States during the interim government......Geographic Reference

item.title: Scrapping referendum verdict violates people’s sovereign will: Rob
item.description: Veteran freedom fighter and president of Jatiya Samajtantrik Dal-JSD president ASM Abdur Rob on Saturday expressed concern over scrapping the referendum verdict in the parliament on Friday......

item.title: All exams to come under unified system to curb irregularities: minister
item.description: Education minister ANM Ehsanul Hoque Milon on Saturday said that the government was working to bring all examinations, including public, recruitment and professional tests, under a single structured and standardised system to ensure accountability and eliminate irregularities......

item.title: RAB preparing list of extortionists, drives to begin soon
item.description: The Rapid Action Battalion is preparing a list of extortionists and will launch nationwide drives to bring them to justice once it is finalised, RAB legal and media wing director MZM Intekhab Chowdhury said on Saturday......

item.title: Man dies in knife attack by cousin sister in Lalmonirhat, two arrested
item.description: A man died on Saturday morning after his cousin sister had allegedly stabbed him the previous night in Lalmonirhat......

item.title: GOP demands state observance of Zafrullah Chowdhury’s death anniv
item.description: Leaders of Gono Odhikar Parishad on Saturday demanded that the death anniversary of Zafrullah Chowdhury be observed at the state level, highlighting his contributions to the country’s health sector and pro-people movements......

item.title: Coast Guard arrests 4 with illegal fuel oil
item.description: The Bangladesh Coast Guard arrested four people with 6,815 litres of illegal fuel oil at Hatiya in Noakhali on Saturday......Bangladesh travel

item.title: 9 held with 5 lakh Yaba pills in Ctg
item.description: Members of Rapid Action Battalion arrested nine drug peddlers and seized five lakh pieces of Yaba tablets worth Tk 15 crore from the engine room of a fishing trawler under Ghat No-15 under Patenga police station in Chattogram city Friday night....

item.title: Coast Guard arrests ‘drug dealers’ with Yaba pills at Teknaf
item.description: The Bangladesh Coast Guard arrested two drug dealers with 1,40,000 Yaba pills worth Tk 7 crore at Teknaf in Cox’s Bazar on Friday......

item.title: Minister calls for coordinated efforts to ensure internet for all
item.description: Posts, telecommunications and information technology and science and technology minister Fakir Mahbub Anam has said that the government is working to ensure internet for all......

item.title: Coordinated reforms stressed for Rajshahi silk revival
item.description: Stakeholders at a meeting on the country’s silk industry on Saturday underscored coordinated policy support, technological upgrading and climate-adaptive production system to revive the once-thriving silk industry in Rajshahi......

item.title: Complaint filed over mob attack calling group homosexual
item.description: A complaint was filed with the Shahbagh police early Saturday over launching a mob attack on a group of least eight people, including a transgender, at Shahbagh on Friday night......

item.title: UIU holds inaugural ceremony of 8th ICSD 2026
item.description: The United International University held the inaugural ceremony of the 8th International Conference on Sustainable Development on the UIU campus at the United City, Madani Avenue in Dhaka on Saturday......Geographic Reference

item.title: BMU VC for increasing public awareness
item.description: The Bangladesh Medical University observed World Parkinson’s Day at the BMU in Dhaka on Saturday......

item.title: Shopping complex fire controlled in hour
item.description: A fire broke out at a shop on the ground floor of Chittagong Shopping Complex at gate no 2 in the Sholoshahar area of Chattogram on Saturday morning......

item.title: School official arrested over raping girl in Kushtia
item.description: THE police on Saturday arrested an official of a secondary school in Mirpur upazila of Kushtia in connection with the alleged rape of a sixth-grade student on the school premises......

item.title: 6 businesses fined for soya bean oil irregularities in Bogura
item.description: Six business establishments at Rajabazar in Bogura were fined Tk 35,000 on Saturday during a joint enforcement drive over irregularities in the sale of open and bottled soybean oil......

item.title: Health minister made EDCL’s reconstituted board chair
item.description: The board of directors of Essential Drugs Company Limited has been reconstituted with the health and family welfare minister Sardar Md Sakhawat Hossain as its chairman......

item.title: Canal encroachers’ list being prepared: state minister
item.description: State minister for local government, rural development and cooperatives Mir Shahe Alam on Saturday said all canals in Dhaka will be recovered and a list of encroachers is being prepared to take legal action against them......

item.title: Veteran journalist ASM Habibullah dies
item.description: Veteran journalist and former chief news editor of Bangladesh Sangbad Sangstha ASM Habibullah died of old-age complications at his residence in Mymensingh on Saturday. He was 91......Bangladesh travel

item.title: Easter truce between Russia, Ukraine begins
item.description: A temporary truce between Russia and Ukraine entered into force on Saturday, with Kyiv warning it would respond ‘immediately’ if Russia violated it......
